Question: Are information security roles and responsibilities for the entire workforce established? Are all workforce members aware of and understand their role in information security? (logging off, securing patient records, etc.)
Plain English: Does everyone at Millie know what they personally need to do to keep patient data safe?
Proposed Answer: Yes. Millie's required Privacy & Security training covers workforce responsibilities (minimum-necessary access, breach reporting, device/password security, encryption, screen locking, secure document handling) and the 01d. Safeguards Policy Policy assigns workforce-level safeguards. Workforce training acknowledgement is collected per 01d. Safeguards Policy §IV.
Gap Description: Confirm training acknowledgement process and refresh evidence (next training cycle).
Required Fix - Text: Refresh / finalize workforce security roles & responsibilities document and attach training acknowledgement records.

Question: Are information security roles and responsibilities for third-party stakeholders established?
Plain English: Do our vendors know what they're responsible for protecting?
Proposed Answer: Yes. The 06a. Business Associate Agreement Policy requires a BAA before any vendor creates, receives, maintains, or transmits PHI; the Supporting Sources/Millie Matrix of Platforms, Software Subscriptions, and Access (1).xlsx identifies third-party systems, access setup owners, license model, and admins.

Question: Are platforms (hardware, devices, applications, software) prioritized based on the information they process, operational criticality, and business value?
Plain English: If everything went down at once, do you know which systems to bring back first?
Proposed Answer: The Supporting Sources/Millie Matrix of Platforms, Software Subscriptions, and Access (1).xlsx is an application/platform inventory (priority column exists), and the 04a. Contingency Planning Policy requires an IT inventory, but a documented recovery-priority order (RTO/RPO per system) is not yet formalized. User-stated software priority: backend, mobile app, admin/frontend.
Gap Description: No formal Critical Systems & Recovery Priority Matrix exists.
Required Fix - Text: Create a Critical Systems & Recovery Priority Matrix (systems × sensitivity × criticality × owner × RTO/RPO × restart order).

Question: 10) Has your organization implemented a security policy? (If so, please attach)
Plain English: Do you have a written security policy?
Proposed Answer: Yes. Millie maintains a consolidated policy suite including 01a. Information Security Framework, 01c. Risk Management Policy, 01d. Safeguards Policy, 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations, 04a. Contingency Planning Policy, 05a. Incident & Breach Response, 06a. Business Associate Agreement Policy, 06b. SDLC & Asset Lifecycle Policy, 02c. PHI Use, Disclosure, Minimum Necessary & De-Identification, 02b. Patient Rights — Access, Amendment & Accounting of Disclosures, 04b. Paper Document Management Policy, plus the 04c. COVID Policy and 04d. Medical Waste Management Plan.

Question: 11) Are information security roles & responsibilities documented in writing and communicated throughout the organization?
Plain English: Is it written down who's responsible for security, and does everyone know who that is?
Proposed Answer: Yes. Workforce training identifies the Chief Privacy Officer and Chief Security Officer; 01c. Risk Management Policy and 05a. Incident & Breach Response explicitly assign responsibilities to those roles plus the Risk Review Committee. 01b. HIPAA Definitions defines the Risk Management Team membership.
Gap Description: Job descriptions weren't reviewed; only assert role responsibilities, not that they live in JDs (unless HR confirms).
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Security & Privacy Roles / RACI addendum referencing HR job descriptions.

Question: 12) Are legal and regulatory requirements regarding securing information, including privacy and civil liberties obligations, understood and managed?
Plain English: Do you follow HIPAA and other healthcare privacy laws?
Proposed Answer: Yes. The HIPAA policy suite addresses Privacy Rule (use & disclosure, patient rights, marketing, accounting), Breach Notification, Minimum Necessary, De-Identification, Definitions, and Safeguards. Citations: 02c. PHI Use, Disclosure, Minimum Necessary & De-Identification, 02b. Patient Rights — Access, Amendment & Accounting of Disclosures, §II of 05a. Incident & Breach Response, 01b. HIPAA Definitions, 01d. Safeguards Policy.

Question: Are you identifying risks to your IT Infrastructure by conducting vulnerability scans and an Enterprise Risk Assessment?
Plain English: Are you regularly scanning for security weaknesses and assessing risks?
Proposed Answer: Yes. Millie is continuously monitored via ConnectSecure (Tenisi-owned tenant) with scans twice monthly — once after Patch Tuesday and once two weeks later to verify remediations. The 01c. Risk Management Policy requires annual enterprise risk assessment; the latest annual risk letter is in Supporting Sources/Copy of Annual Risk Letter _ Mitigations from 03q HIPAA - Risk Management Policy.docx.

Question: What was the date of your last Risk Assessment?
Plain English: When was your last security risk review?
Proposed Answer: The most recent vulnerability scan was performed on 4/30/2026 (Tenisi/ConnectSecure). The most recent annual risk letter is filed in Supporting Sources/Copy of Annual Risk Letter _ Mitigations from 03q HIPAA - Risk Management Policy.docx.

Question: Are threats, both internal and external, identified and documented in the risk assessment?
Plain English: Does your risk review list out specific threats — both people inside the company and outsiders?
Proposed Answer: The 01c. Risk Management Policy §III requires threat identification (Human-Intentional, Human-Unintentional, Environment-Natural, Environment-Man Made), vulnerability identification, control analysis, likelihood, impact, and risk determination. Threat-register evidence is in Supporting Sources/Copy of Annual Risk Letter _ Mitigations from 03q HIPAA - Risk Management Policy.docx.
Gap Description: Confirm current risk assessment / threat register attaches all four threat categories with named threats.
Required Fix - Text: Attach current threat register showing internal/external threats by category, vulnerabilities, controls, likelihood, impact, residual risk, owner.

Question: Who is responsible for performing the risk analysis?
Plain English: Who actually does the risk review?
Proposed Answer: The Risk Review Committee (Chief Privacy Officer, Chief Security Officer, subject-matter experts), per 01c. Risk Management Policy §III. 01b. HIPAA Definitions also defines the Risk Management Team (compliance, security, privacy, HR/facilities, technology SMEs). Tenisitech owns the ConnectSecure tenant and performs the vulnerability-scan portion.

Question: Is there a policy defining organizational risk tolerance and risk acceptance authority?
Plain English: Do you have a written rule about who decides which risks are acceptable to live with?
Proposed Answer: The 01c. Risk Management Policy requires reporting risk-assessment results to the Risk Review Committee and (for material risks) the Board or designated committee, but does not define explicit risk-tolerance thresholds or formal acceptance authority.
Gap Description: No explicit Risk Acceptance / Risk Tolerance policy.
Required Fix - Text: Append 1-paragraph clause to Risk Management Policy: Risk acceptance authority is held by the Chief Security Officer for operational risks; material risks must be accepted in writing by the Chief Executive Officer with Board notification. Acceptance is documented in the risk register with rationale, compensating controls, and review cadence.

Question: Is there a facility security plan that manages and protects all assets?
Plain English: Do you have a written plan for keeping the clinic building physically secure?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 01d. Safeguards Policy §III covers Facility Access Controls and physical safeguards; 04b. Paper Document Management Policy covers paper-PHI handling and secure storage.

Question: How do you control remote access to your network?
Plain English: How do you let people log in safely from outside the clinic?
Proposed Answer: There is currently no remote access via VPN services. Tenisitech uses NinjaOne RMM to remotely access devices as needed for troubleshooting. The 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations §III (Remote Access) defines requirements where remote access is used (authorization, encryption, no-local-PHI, monitoring, termination of privileges).

Question: Are access permissions managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties?
Plain English: Do people only get access to what they need for their job?
Proposed Answer: Yes. Millie's internal admin dashboard enforces RBAC based on role; AWS, Aptible, and GitHub use RBAC and are limited to the technical team. Supported by §II of 02c. PHI Use, Disclosure, Minimum Necessary & De-Identification (Minimum Necessary Rule) and 01d. Safeguards Policy (unique user IDs by job responsibility).
Gap Description: Could strengthen with screenshots / admin RBAC role matrix.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Admin RBAC role matrix screenshot.

Question: Do you segment your network into protected/isolated networks?
Plain English: Are your patient-data systems on a separate network from general office WiFi?
Proposed Answer: There is no physical server infrastructure (cloud-only). LAN is separate from production. Wireless is split into guest and production networks. Tenisi-owned operational control.
Gap Description: No standalone Network Segmentation diagram on file.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Network Segmentation / Architecture Statement (1 page summary + simple diagram).

Question: Do you track and measure user training completion?
Plain English: Do you keep records of who finished the security training?
Proposed Answer: The Privacy & Security Training (March 2025) document requires every employee to complete training and includes an acknowledgment. A formal completion tracker / LMS export / measurement (test) is not currently on file.
Gap Description: Need evidence that completion is tracked and measured.
Required Fix - Text: Create Training Completion Tracking SOP + roster/export (assignment, completion, reminders, acknowledgement retention).

Question: Do you have a formal vendor management program that requires a minimum level of HIPAA compliance based on risk?
Plain English: Do you have a real process for checking vendors' security before working with them?
Proposed Answer: The 06a. Business Associate Agreement Policy requires BAAs before any vendor handles PHI, and the Supporting Sources/Millie Matrix of Platforms, Software Subscriptions, and Access (1).xlsx identifies vendor admins and BAA status. A formal risk-tiered vendor management program (security questionnaire, scoring, periodic reassessment) is not yet documented.
Gap Description: BAA process exists; vendor security questionnaire/risk tiering evidence not formalized.
Required Fix - Text: Create Vendor Risk Management Program SOP (vendor inventory, risk tier, HIPAA/security questionnaire, BAA review, approval workflow, periodic reassessment) + evidence folder.

Question: What technical controls are being used to protect data stored on electronic storage media?
Plain English: How is patient data protected on hard drives, databases, and storage?
Proposed Answer: Password protection, unique user IDs, 2FA where possible, approved password manager usage, device encryption for any device touching PHI, prohibitions on local PHI storage, and encryption for ePHI over open networks (per §I/§II/§III/§IV of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations). Aptible-managed databases are encrypted at rest (https://www.aptible.com/docs/core-concepts/managed-databases/managing-databases/database-encryption/overview).

Question: What physical security controls are in place to protect data?
Plain English: What physical things (locks, cabinets, etc.) protect patient data at the clinic?
Proposed Answer: Per 01d. Safeguards Policy (Safeguards Policy) §III and 04b. Paper Document Management Policy (Paper Document Management), Millie uses locked storage for paper PHI, restricted-access work areas, visitor controls, and workstation locking. §IV of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations (Workstation Use) requires session locking and prohibits local PHI storage.

Question: How is data protected when it is in transit to external organizations?
Plain English: How is patient data kept safe when emailed or sent to others?
Proposed Answer: ePHI must be encrypted before email or transmission over open networks (§I of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations — Encryption, requiring FIPS 140-2 / NIST SP 800-52/77/113 standards). The Remote Access section (§III of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations) prohibits transmitting PHI over unencrypted channels.

Question: Are assets formally managed throughout the life cycle (acquisition, movement, transfers, disposal)?
Plain English: Is there a documented process for buying, moving, and getting rid of computers and equipment?
Proposed Answer: 04a. Contingency Planning Policy requires maintaining and updating an IT inventory and prohibits purchasing IT components without Chief Security Officer approval; §III of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations (Remote Access) requires tracking removed Company equipment; §IV (Workstation Use) and 04b. Paper Document Management Policy address secure storage and disposal. 06b. SDLC & Asset Lifecycle Policy (SDLC) covers asset-lifecycle handling for software/systems.
Gap Description: Hardware lifecycle for Tenisi-managed devices isn't end-to-end documented in Millie's policies.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Asset Lifecycle SOP / Tenisi hardware inventory export.

Question: How are you monitoring your networks for data leaks?
Plain English: How do you watch for someone leaking data out of the company?
Proposed Answer: Audit logging across the data stack: Fivetran (connector / sync activity), Databricks (logins, data access, queries), Hex (workspace actions/access), Aptible (workspace actions, database access), AWS (CloudTrail + CloudWatch for app activity). User Notifications in AWS alert on sign-in anomalies and root-user logins. Anomalous-access investigations are documented in incident logs per §I of 05a (Incident & Breach Response).
Gap Description: No standalone DLP/log-monitoring statement; reliance on multiple per-platform audit logs.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Data Leak Monitoring / DLP Statement (1 page) summarizing per-platform audit logging, review cadence, and escalation.

Question: Is a baseline configuration of IT systems created and maintained?
Plain English: Do you have a standard setup that every computer/server starts from?
Proposed Answer: Devices enrolled in NinjaOne (Tenisi RMM) receive patch-management schedules along with baseline security tools, vulnerability-scanning agent (ConnectSecure), and policy management. §IV of 03a (Workstation Use) defines baseline expectations.
Gap Description: No documented Secure Configuration Baseline Standard outside Tenisi's NinjaOne configuration.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Secure Configuration Baseline Standard (endpoint / server / cloud / network baseline controls, owner, review cadence, exceptions).

Question: Is a System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) implemented for all asset and hardware configurations?
Plain English: Is there a documented step-by-step lifecycle (plan, build, test, deploy, retire) for systems?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 06b. SDLC & Asset Lifecycle Policy (SDLC & Asset Lifecycle Policy) defines the lifecycle for systems and software (planning, requirements, design, testing, implementation, operations/maintenance, retirement). AGENTS.md adds engineering change discipline, testing, approvals.

Question: Are configuration change control processes in place?
Plain English: Is there a real process to track, review, and approve changes to systems?
Proposed Answer: 06b. SDLC & Asset Lifecycle Policy §III(c) covers pre-deploy validation and approval gates; engineering changes use GitHub PR review + Aptible deploy logs. A discretely-named Change Management policy/log with classification (standard / normal / emergency) is not yet on file.
Gap Description: Need formal Change Management Policy with change-log template.
Required Fix - Text: Append 1-paragraph Change Management section to SDLC Policy: Changes to production systems require peer review and approval through GitHub pull-request + Aptible deploy pipeline. Chief Security Officer owns the change-control process; reviewed annually. Production-change records are retained via Git history and Aptible deploy logs.

Question: Are backups of information and server/laptop configurations created, maintained, and tested periodically?
Plain English: Do you back up patient data and computer settings, and do you test the backups?
Proposed Answer: Yes for data and software. 04a. Contingency Planning Policy requires backup of all data on the IT System every 24 hours, encrypted off-site storage, quarterly review of backup procedures, and DR drills/tests. The May 2026 DR review is in Supporting Sources/Disaster Recovery Review - May 2026.xlsx. No backup software is in place for laptops; no on-prem server infrastructure exists (SaaS only).

Question: Is there a disposal policy that addresses both data and hardware?
Plain English: Is there a written rule for how to safely throw away patient data and old computers?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 01d. Safeguards Policy requires PHI disposal in a manner that renders it unreadable/unusable, paper shredding, media sanitization before reuse, and ePHI deletion when no longer needed. 04b. Paper Document Management Policy requires shredding of protected documents and vendor certificates of destruction.

Question: Are Business Continuity plans and Disaster Recovery plans in place and managed?
Plain English: Do you have a written plan for keeping the clinic running after a disaster?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 04a. Contingency Planning Policy establishes continuity/disaster response, data and software backup, IT inventory, UPS/power protections, DR plan preparation/approval, critical-provider business-continuity clauses, emergency operations, drills/testing, updates, confidentiality, and insurance considerations. The May 2026 DR review (in Supporting Sources/Disaster Recovery Review - May 2026.xlsx) is the latest evidence.

Question: Do you conduct background checks on all members of the workforce, and exit interviews to cover HIPAA obligations after employment?
Plain English: Do you check new hires' backgrounds and remind them about confidentiality when they leave?
Proposed Answer: Background checks and exit interviews are HR-managed via Gusto. No formal Millie-side policy / HR attestation documenting that HIPAA-confidentiality reminders are part of the exit process.
Gap Description: HR-owned process not formally documented as a security policy.
Required Fix - Text: HR to add: background-check scope and timing, exit checklist including HIPAA confidentiality reminder, access handoff to IT. Capture as HR SOP or 1-paragraph attestation.

Question: Is there a formal process for provisioning and removal of all user accounts, and physical access?
Plain English: Is there a documented process for creating and removing user accounts and building access?
Proposed Answer: §II/§III of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations (Password / Remote Access) requires deactivation of passwords and remote access on termination or role change. Supporting Sources/Millie Matrix of Platforms, Software Subscriptions, and Access (1).xlsx captures per-platform admins. A formal cross-system access provisioning/deprovisioning SOP covering all user accounts AND physical access is not yet on file.
Gap Description: Need formal user lifecycle / access request workflow.
Required Fix - Text: Create Access Provisioning & Deprovisioning SOP (request, approval, role mapping, least privilege, provisioning, periodic review, termination/offboarding SLAs, physical access).

Question: Do you restrict the use of removable media (USB drives) and limit access to only authorized users?
Plain English: Are USB sticks and external drives controlled and limited to approved people?
Proposed Answer: Yes. §IV of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations (Workstation Use) requires work files to be stored on the external hosting platform or app — not on C drive, CD, thumb drive, or directly on mobile devices unless authorized. §III (Remote Access) prohibits storing PHI on hard drives. 01d. Safeguards Policy requires ePHI removal from media before reuse.

Question: Have you mapped all internal and external data flows?
Plain English: Do you know exactly where patient data lives and who you share it with?
Proposed Answer: AGENTS.md describes major systems and workflows (Acuity, EMR/Elation, Zendesk, Zentake, Withings, Customer.io, Twilio/SendGrid, AWS/Aptible). The Supporting Sources/Millie Matrix of Platforms, Software Subscriptions, and Access (1).xlsx identifies systems and access. No complete internal/external sensitive-data-flow map with PHI categories, sources, destinations, vendors, and owners.
Gap Description: No explicit data-flow map / data inventory document.
Required Fix - Text: Create Internal/External Data Flow Map and Data Inventory (system inventory, PHI categories, source/destination, transfers, vendors, storage locations, encryption, owner, retention, BAAs).

Question: Are security/privacy incidents reviewed for impact?
Plain English: After a security event, do you review what happened and what damage was done?
Proposed Answer: Yes. §I of 05a. Incident & Breach Response (Security Incident Management) requires prompt reporting, CSO investigation within 24 hours, response priorities, incident logs, post-incident analysis, and annual reporting. §II (Breach Notification) requires investigation, PHI compromise assessment, risk assessment, notifications, mitigation, sanctions, and accounting of disclosures.

Question: Do you have an incident management policy that defines criticality and decision response authority?
Plain English: Do you have a written policy that says how serious each incident is and who decides what to do?
Proposed Answer: Yes. §I of 05a. Incident & Breach Response assigns incident response authority to the Chief Security Officer, Chief Compliance Officer, Chief Privacy Officer, General Counsel/CLO, and CEO depending on incident actions. §II identifies the Chief Privacy Officer as responsible for breach investigation/notification decisions with Security Officer involvement.
Gap Description: Severity-tier categories aren't explicit in the policy.
Required Fix - Text: Optional: Incident Severity & Escalation Matrix referenced from the Incident & Breach Response Policy.

Question: Is the network monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events?
Plain English: Are you watching your network for hackers and unusual activity?
Proposed Answer: §III of 03a. Technical Safeguards — Encryption, Passwords, Remote Access & Workstations (Remote Access) mentions maintaining/reviewing records of inbound remote access. AWS GuardDuty and User Notifications alert on sign-in anomalies. No formal IDS/IPS / SIEM / firewall-alerting documentation outside Tenisi's NinjaOne agent.
Gap Description: No standalone Network Security Monitoring statement; relies on Tenisi-managed agents + per-platform alerts.
Required Fix - Text: Create Network Security Monitoring / IDS-IPS Statement (coverage, Tenisi alerting, review cadence, escalation, sample alert evidence).

Question: Is the physical environment monitored to detect potential security events? (cameras, security guards, badge readers)
Plain English: Do you have cameras or badge readers watching the clinic for break-ins?
Proposed Answer: 01d. Safeguards Policy includes visitor controls, IDs, and secure storage. Cameras / guards / badge readers / equivalent active physical-environment monitoring per-site is not documented in policy.
Gap Description: Site-by-site physical monitoring details not documented.
Required Fix - Text: Create per-site Physical Security Monitoring Statement (locks, cameras, badge/access controls, visitor logs, restricted areas, responsible owner, exceptions).

Question: Do you have a security/privacy incident response policy that identifies key personnel and requires root cause analysis?
Plain English: Do you have a written incident response policy that requires figuring out the root cause?
Proposed Answer: Yes. §I of 05a. Incident & Breach Response identifies key personnel, reporting, investigation, response priorities, incident logs, and post-incident analysis (vulnerabilities exploited, how the incident occurred, resolution/containment, policy adequacy, unresolved vulnerabilities, needed updates). §II covers breach determination and risk assessment.

Question: Do you test your down time procedures and your disaster recovery plans?
Plain English: Do you actually practice your disaster recovery plan?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 04a. Contingency Planning Policy requires the Chief Security Officer to conduct drills and exercises with personnel to test and evaluate DR plans, and submit written summaries to the CEO. The May 2026 DR review in Supporting Sources/Disaster Recovery Review - May 2026.xlsx is the latest test evidence.

Question: Do you incorporate lessons learned into the disaster recovery planning process?
Plain English: After a disaster drill, do you update your plan based on what you learned?
Proposed Answer: Yes. 04a. Contingency Planning Policy states that DR plans and emergency protocols must be revised to remedy deficiencies identified during drills, or to reflect material changes in operations, systems, personnel, contractors, and business arrangements.
